articles.headline: India GDP growth forecast raised to 7.2 percent for FY25
articles.summary: Major financial agencies and the RBI have upgraded India’s GDP growth projection to 7.2% for the current fiscal year. The upgrade reflects strong industrial performance, consistent urban demand, and a recovery in rural consumption. The government’s focus on capital expenditure and infrastructure development continues to drive economic momentum despite global volatility.

articles.headline: RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5 percent to curb inflation
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s Monetary Policy Committee has voted to keep the policy repo rate unchanged at 6.5%. Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted that while economic growth remains resilient, the central bank must remain vigilant regarding food inflation. The decision aligns with the goal of achieving a sustainable 4% inflation target while supporting domestic economic activity.

articles.headline: Sensex hits record high crossing the historic 80,000 milestone
articles.summary: The Indian equity markets reached a historic high as the BSE Sensex crossed the 80,000 mark for the first time. The rally was driven by heavy buying in banking and IT stocks, coupled with positive global cues and strong institutional inflows. Investors remain optimistic about India's macroeconomic stability and the continuity of policy reforms after the general elections.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves infrastructure projects worth Rs 50,000 crore
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet has cleared multiple infrastructure projects with a total outlay of Rs 50,000 crore. These include new highway corridors, railway station redevelopments, and port connectivity improvements. The move is expected to enhance logistical efficiency, reduce transport costs, and create significant employment opportunities across several states in the country.

articles.headline: TCS reports 5 percent rise in net profit for Q1
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services reported a 5% year-on-year growth in net profit to Rs 12,040 crore for the June quarter. The IT major’s revenue also increased, led by strong performance in the BFSI and healthcare segments. Despite a cautious global environment, the company noted a robust deal pipeline and steady demand for cloud and AI transformation services.

articles.headline: US Federal Reserve signals potential rate cuts in upcoming months
articles.summary: US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated that the central bank is nearing the confidence level needed to begin cutting interest rates. While inflation remains above the 2% target, recent data shows a cooling trend in the labor market. Global markets responded positively to the hints, anticipating a shift in US monetary policy before the end of the year.

articles.headline: Supreme Court seeks report on implementation of Green Credit rules
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has sought a detailed report from the Center regarding the implementation of the Green Credit Program. The court expressed concerns over potential greenwashing and the transparency of carbon credit calculations. This legal review aims to ensure that environmental policies effectively contribute to conservation without compromising indigenous forest rights.

articles.headline: India successfully tests long range hypersonic missile off Odisha coast
articles.summary: The DRDO successfully conducted a flight trial of a long-range hypersonic missile from Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam Island. The missile performed high-level maneuvers and reached the designated target with precision. This test marks a significant milestone in India's indigenous defense technology, placing the country among a select group of nations with such advanced capabilities.

articles.headline: India defeats Sri Lanka in opening T20 international cricket match
articles.summary: The Indian cricket team secured a comprehensive 43-run victory over Sri Lanka in the series opener. After setting a challenging target of 214, the Indian bowlers restricted the hosts through clinical death bowling and disciplined spin. This match marks a successful start for the new coaching staff and reinforces India’s dominant position in the T20 format.

articles.headline: Education ministry launches AI tool for regional language learning
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education has launched an AI-based tool designed to translate educational content into 22 regional languages. The initiative aims to bridge the language gap in higher education and provide students in rural areas with access to high-quality technical resources in their native tongues, aligning with the goals of the National Education Policy.

articles.headline: India Inc Q4 Earnings Weather Geopolitical Storm with Healthy Growth
articles.summary: India Inc reported 14% revenue growth and 18% adjusted PAT growth for Q4FY26 despite the West Asia conflict. Excluding BFSI, revenue growth hit 17%. Capital goods led sectors with 43% revenue and 68% PAT growth. Challenges remain for FMCG and IT due to supply chain disruptions and rising raw material costs if the conflict persists beyond the March quarter.

articles.headline: Centre Tightens Labour Compliance Rules for All Government Contractors
articles.summary: The Department of Expenditure has introduced a procurement-linked compliance framework for outsourced and contractual workers. Firms seeking government contracts must ensure timely wage payments and statutory social security contributions. Violators face a government-wide debarment for up to three years. Ministries must now monitor wage disbursements through bank transfers every month.

articles.headline: SAMHI Hotels Reaches Profitability with Attractive Valuations Post-Correction
articles.summary: Branded hotel owner SAMHI Hotels has transitioned from IPO-era losses to a trailing twelve-month profit of ₹216 crore. Total debt has decreased from ₹2,690 crore to ₹1,726 crore. The company is pivoting toward a capital-light model by acquiring a 70% stake in luxury platform RARE India. Analysts suggest accumulating on dips as the stock trades at 14.6 times FY27 earnings.

articles.headline: Global Oil Supply Gap Reaches 900 Million Barrels Amid Conflict
articles.summary: Crude oil supply losses have hit 904 million barrels since the US-Iran conflict began, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining unnavigable. Dated Brent has corrected 27% from its $144 peak to $101, but analysts warn of a transition from a 'flow' problem to a 'stock' problem as global inventories deplete at a rate of 8 million barrels per day. Technical upside remains toward $140.

articles.headline: Motilal Oswal Launches New Contrarian Investment Strategy Mutual Fund
articles.summary: Motilal Oswal Mutual Fund has opened the New Fund Offer (NFO) for its Contra Fund. The open-ended equity scheme will invest against prevailing market trends and is benchmarked against the Nifty 500 Total Return Index. The NFO period concludes on May 22. A 1% exit load applies if redeemed within 365 days of allotment.

articles.headline: Invesco Mutual Fund Resumes Subscriptions for Three International Schemes
articles.summary: Invesco Mutual Fund has revoked the temporary suspension of subscriptions for its Global Equity Income, Pan European Equity, and Global Consumer Trends funds of funds. The move follows available headroom under overseas investment limits set in 2022. Fresh registrations for SIP, STP, and lumpsum purchases are now permitted.

articles.headline: Edelweiss Aggressive Hybrid Fund Maintains Strong 7-Year Performance
articles.summary: Edelweiss Aggressive Hybrid Fund has delivered a 14.4% CAGR over seven years, outperforming the category average of 12.5%. The fund uses a factor-driven approach combining growth, quality, and momentum. Currently, it is overweight in pharma and capital goods while holding a conservative debt portfolio with a yield to maturity of 6.7%.

articles.headline: Rising Cost of Equity Signals Potential Market Risks and Complacency
articles.summary: The theoretical cost of equity for Indian markets has risen from 10.8% in January to 11.7% in April 2026. Higher interest rates and geopolitical uncertainty are creating an earnings growth overhang. Analysts suggest that current Nifty P/E levels of 22x may be underpricing risks compared to past cycles when the cost of equity was lower.

articles.headline: Global CEOs Focus on India Growth Across Manufacturing and Energy
articles.summary: Quarterly earnings calls from global giants highlight India as a strategic hub. Monster Beverage saw 94% sales growth in India, while AB InBev and Marriott noted steady expansion. Schneider Electric and Wallenius Wilhelmsen identify the nation as an emerging R&D and heavy equipment export hub. Cameco and Transocean point to strong multi-year demand in uranium and offshore drilling.

articles.headline: Ramco Cements Faces Margin Pressures Despite Planned Capacity Expansion
articles.summary: The Ramco Cements plans to increase capacity to 31 mtpa by FY28. However, the company faces margin headwinds from a 20% mineral land tax in Tamil Nadu and rising coking coal costs due to West Asia conflicts. While 9MFY26 revenue rose 5%, analysts recommend a 'hold' as current valuations at 40x forward earnings reflect a significant premium to its five-year average.

articles.headline: Nifty Bullish Outlook Sustained as Futures Data Shows Long Build-up
articles.summary: Nifty futures for May held the 24,000 support level last week, seeing open interest rise to 171 lakh contracts. The Put-Call Ratio of 1.10 indicates bullish sentiment. If 25,000 is surpassed, Nifty could target 26,000. For Nifty Bank, the outlook remains positive as long as the 55,000 base holds, with an eventual recovery target of 59,000.

articles.headline: PFRDA Clarifies Maintenance Charges for Inactive and Tier II NPS Accounts
articles.summary: PFRDA has announced that annual maintenance charges (AMC) for NPS Tier II accounts will align with Tier I rates starting October 1, 2025. Accounts with a corpus below ₹1,000 will be exempt from AMC. Dormant accounts—defined as those with no contributions for four consecutive quarters—will only pay 10% of the standard maintenance fee.

articles.headline: NPS Sanchay Launched to Provide Simplified Pension Access for Informal Workers
articles.summary: The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) has unveiled 'NPS Sanchay', a simplified variant of the National Pension System. Targeted at India's informal workforce, it removes technical asset allocation hurdles for last-mile subscribers. Eligibility for the scheme extends to all Indian citizens between the ages of 18 and 85.

articles.headline: April e-Way Bill Generation Grows 12% Despite West Asia Crisis
articles.summary: India's e-Way bill generation reached 13.34 crore in April, a 12% year-on-year increase. Although volume dipped 5% from March's all-time high due to seasonal fiscal year-end factors, the data indicates resilient underlying economic activity and tax compliance. Experts suggest the seasonal cooling-off does not signify a slowdown in domestic consumption demand.

articles.headline: Vijay to be TN commander-in-chief, numbers stack up
articles.summary: C Joseph Vijay invited to form Tamil Nadu government after TVK secured support of 120 MLAs. Governor directed CM-designate to seek vote of confidence by May 13. Swearing-in ceremony to be held on May 10.

articles.headline: Hormuz calm, but Iran, US far from breakthrough deal
articles.summary: Relative calm prevailed around the Strait of Hormuz as US awaited Iran's response to proposals to end conflict. Sporadic flare-ups were reported, with US military striking Iranian forces.

articles.headline: 70-year-old dies after fire at Ghaziabad AC service centre
articles.summary: A 70-year-old man died in a fire at an AC service centre in Ghaziabad, likely caused by a short-circuit. At least 12 vehicles were gutted in the blaze.

articles.headline: Andhra CJ unveils court complex in East Godavari
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh High Court Chief Justice Lisa Gill inaugurated the 'Ten Courts Building Complex' in East Godavari district, attended by judges.

articles.headline: Kerala cops issue alert about online mattress fraud
articles.summary: Kerala police warned of a new cyber fraud targeting mattress buyers. Fraudsters pose as company representatives, tricking customers into making repeated payments.

articles.headline: Four killed after car hits lorry in Karnataka
articles.summary: Four people died in a head-on collision between a car and a lorry in Karnataka's Tumakuru district. The accident occurred when the car collided with an oncoming lorry.

articles.headline: AIADMK leaders' polldiary fuels talk of rift
articles.summary: AIADMK may face a split as 36 legislators expressed support for TVK. AIADMK faction accused TVK of forging letters. Party members met separately, fueling speculation.

articles.headline: Suvendu joins modest cohort of non-RSS CMs
articles.summary: Suvendu Adhikari, a former TMC confidant, joins non-RSS CMs in BJP's history. His past association with TMC and grassroots politics were credited for BJP's outreach.

articles.headline: Ghose slams BJP MPs’ absence at Tagore tribute
articles.summary: TMC MP Sagarika Ghose criticized BJP MPs for absence in Parliament during Rabindranath Tagore's birth anniversary tribute. Union minister Kiren Rijiju hit back, citing parliamentary practices.

articles.headline: EC-appointed poll observer chosen as advisor to Bengal CM
articles.summary: Subrata Gupta, an EC-appointed special observer, has been chosen as advisor to West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari. He is a retired IAS officer with vast experience.

articles.headline: 3 arrested for unrelated cyber frauds of ₹1.2cr
articles.summary: Three individuals from Gujarat, Rajasthan, and MP arrested for providing mule bank accounts for ₹1.2cr in cyber fraud, including fake IPO investments and forex trading scams.

articles.headline: 23 officials reshuffled across key Delhi departments
articles.summary: Delhi government reshuffled 23 IAS and DANICS officers across key departments. Navin Kumar Choudhary assigned additional charge as director of information and publicity.

articles.headline: Woman abducted by Myanmar unit rescued: Officials
articles.summary: Assam Rifles rescued a Naga woman abducted by Myanmar's KNA-B faction and VVEZ. The woman was released safely after joint efforts by security forces.

articles.headline: SC must not apply SC/ST Act bail bar mechanically: SC
articles.summary: Supreme Court held that anticipatory bail cannot be mechanically denied under SC/ST Act. Courts must scrutinize allegations and facts in each case before refusing relief.

articles.headline: Won’t roll back sacrilege law: CM after ultimatum
articles.summary: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann refused to roll back the sacrilege law despite an Akal Takht ultimatum. He asserted the law has overwhelming support and stricter punishment is needed.

articles.headline: Husband among 2 held in '92 murder case after DNA confirms identity
articles.summary: A man and his brother arrested for a 1992 murder after DNA confirmed skeletal remains belonged to the wife. The murder was pre-planned due to frequent fights.

articles.headline: Hospital staffer held for raping 16-yr-old in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A hospital staffer arrested for sedating and raping a 16-year-old girl in Karnataka's Vijayanagar district. The accused allegedly lured her away from the hospital.

articles.headline: Sawan Barwal breaks 48-yr-old national record in marathon
articles.summary: Sawan Barwal broke the 48-year-old national marathon record with 2:11:58 in Rotterdam, qualifying for Asian Games. He had an interest in banking before becoming an athlete.

articles.headline: Against the flame: Forest firefighters' memorials
articles.summary: Raza Kazmi traces memorials to forest firefighters who lost their lives protecting forests, highlighting their courage and devotion to duty across India.

articles.headline: No-holds bard: Poetry collection addresses women's trauma
articles.summary: Sing, Slivered Tongue, an anthology, features 67 poems by South Asian women addressing trauma, loss, and everyday struggles, aiming to give pain a place to exist.

articles.headline: Will, and grace: Euthanasia laws evolve
articles.summary: Charles Assisi explores passive euthanasia and the meaning of 'sanctity of life' as India's laws evolve. He advocates for organ donation and a dignified end.

articles.headline: Delhi Capitals rue not capitalising after another poor season
articles.summary: Delhi Capitals' IPL season thinned out due to missed moments and an inability to capitalise. They sit eighth in the points table after seven losses.

articles.headline: Shedge hopes 'scoring switch' will need explosive prep
articles.summary: Suryansh Shedge, after observing from the dugout, made impactful contributions for Punjab Kings. He focused on mental preparation and adaptability for various batting situations.

articles.headline: WFI showcauses Vinesh, keeps her out of tourney
articles.summary: WFI issued a showcause notice to Vinesh Phogat, barring her from a tournament due to disqualification issues and 'grave acts of indiscipline'. She may face suspension.

articles.headline: Liverpool frustrated by Chelsea draw, Utd held
articles.summary: Liverpool drew 1-1 with Chelsea, missing a chance for Champions League spot. Manchester United were held to a goalless draw by Sunderland.

articles.headline: Iran reviewing US proposal at ‘own pace’ as Trump awaits response
articles.summary: Iran is reviewing the US proposal related to the West Asia conflict, responding 'at the appropriate time'. US expects a response soon but Iran dismisses ultimatums.

articles.headline: Russia, Kyiv trade accusations of ceasefire violations
articles.summary: Russia and Ukraine accused each other of violating the US-brokered ceasefire. No major strikes were reported, but both sides claimed violations.

articles.headline: US steps up sanctions on Iran
articles.summary: US imposed sanctions on three Chinese firms and individuals for providing satellite imagery and components to Iran's military, restricting technological support.

articles.headline: Elon Musk, Sam Altman’s management styles under fire at OpenAI trial
articles.summary: Testimony in the OpenAI trial criticized Elon Musk for lacking AI technical competency and having a hot temper. Sam Altman faced criticism over leadership and integrity.

articles.headline: 3 Indian-origin UK PM Starmer is challenged by former minister
articles.summary: Keir Starmer faces challenge for Labour leadership from former minister Catherine West after election defeat. West stated she would run if no other hopefuls emerged.

articles.headline: 3 Indian-origin men convicted of murdering former minister couple in Canada
articles.summary: Three Indian-origin individuals convicted of first-degree murder of a couple in Canada. The murders were financially motivated, not a robbery gone wrong.

articles.headline: US: In AI, must avoid dependencies on adversarial nations
articles.summary: US official emphasized openness and avoiding dependencies on adversarial nations for AI development. India and US collaboration on AI was highlighted.

articles.headline: US removes enriched uranium from Venezuela reactor
articles.summary: US removed highly enriched uranium from a research reactor in Venezuela, enhancing nuclear security in South America and the US.

articles.summary: Report on the development of regional economic corridors, aiming to boost trade and connectivity. It covers infrastructure projects, industrial hubs, and cross-border cooperation.

articles.headline: Local Political Engagements
articles.summary: An overview of local political engagements and community dialogues. It covers citizen participation, local governance issues, and resolution of regional concerns.

articles.headline: Cultural Tourism Initiatives
articles.summary: This article details new cultural tourism initiatives promoting local heritage and traditions. It covers festivals, historical sites, and artisan craft villages.

articles.headline: Urban Infrastructure Upgrades
articles.summary: Update on urban infrastructure upgrades, including roads, sanitation, and public spaces. It highlights smart city projects and sustainable development goals.

articles.headline: Science Research Funding
articles.summary: This article discusses increased funding for scientific research and development. It covers grants, academic collaborations, and priority areas for technological advancement.

articles.headline: Sports Talent Development
articles.summary: Report on sports talent development programs nurturing promising athletes. It covers coaching facilities, scholarships, and participation in national and international events.

articles.headline: Environmental Compliance Reporting
articles.summary: Update on environmental compliance reporting and enforcement. It covers industry regulations, pollution control measures, and monitoring of ecological standards.

articles.headline: Government Transparency Initiatives
articles.summary: This article discusses new government transparency initiatives and anti-corruption measures. It covers public access to information and accountability reforms.

articles.headline: Education Access for All
articles.summary: Report on initiatives to ensure education access for all, focusing on marginalized communities. It covers school enrollment, infrastructure, and inclusive learning environments.

articles.headline: National Heritage Trust Projects
articles.summary: Report on projects undertaken by the National Heritage Trust for cultural preservation. It covers restoration of monuments and promotion of traditional crafts.

articles.headline: International Peace Efforts
articles.summary: This article outlines international peace efforts and diplomatic initiatives to resolve conflicts. It covers multilateral discussions and humanitarian interventions.

articles.headline: Scientific Research Collaborations
articles.summary: Report on scientific research collaborations between national and international institutions. It highlights joint projects, knowledge exchange, and advancements in various fields.

articles.headline: Sports Development Funds
articles.summary: New funds allocated for sports development and infrastructure. It covers grassroots programs, elite training, and promotion of physical fitness nationwide.

articles.headline: Government Accountability Measures
articles.summary: Report on new government accountability measures and reforms in public administration. It covers efforts to enhance transparency, efficiency, and citizen-centric governance.

articles.headline: Legal System Modernization
articles.summary: An update on the modernization of the legal system, including e-courts and digital case management. It highlights efforts to improve efficiency and access to justice.

articles.headline: Climate Resilience Projects
articles.summary: Report on climate resilience projects and adaptation strategies. It covers initiatives for sustainable agriculture, water management, and disaster risk reduction.

articles.headline: Vijay to take reins today; T.N. set for coalition govt.
articles.summary: C. Joseph Vijay to be sworn in as TN CM, leading a coalition govt. with Congress. Governor appointed Vijay CM-designate after securing support of 120 MLAs-elect. Nine ministers to take oath with Vijay. Governor directed Vijay to seek vote of confidence by May 13.

articles.headline: Delhi govt. declares 673 hectares of Central Ridge land as reserved forest
articles.summary: Delhi govt. declared 673.32 hectares of Central Ridge as 'reserved forest'. CM termed it a 'historic milestone' in environmental conservation. This completes an important process pending for over three decades.

articles.headline: Three killed, four injured in truck-auto collision in Nuh
articles.summary: Three killed, including a child, and four injured in a truck-auto collision on Delhi-Alwar road in Haryana's Nuh district. The victims were returning from a wedding.

articles.headline: Parents accuse police of failing to investigate class teacher’s role in rape
articles.summary: Parents of a 3-year-old rape victim accuse school and police of ignoring class teacher's role. They allege investigators ignored crucial aspects of the case, including the teacher's involvement.

articles.headline: Social media, expensive gifts used by Dinesh Karala gang to recruit, influence youth
articles.summary: Dinesh Karala gang uses social media, expensive gifts, and firearms to recruit youth in Delhi and Haryana. Recruits are drawn into drug use and criminal activities. Police monitor social media for recruitment tactics.

articles.headline: Assam Congress chief takes responsibility for poll defeat
articles.summary: Assam Congress president Gaurav Gogoi accepts moral responsibility for poll defeat. Party leadership will undertake public outreach and hold discussions to address organizational weaknesses and voter concerns.

articles.headline: U.P. CM meets Governor amid Cabinet expansion buzz
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath met Governor Anandiben Patel, fueling speculation of a Cabinet expansion. The CM presented a book to the Governor.

articles.headline: Selja concerned over crime against children in Haryana
articles.summary: Congress general secretary Kumari Selja expressed concern over rising crimes against children in Haryana, citing NCRB data. Haryana recorded the highest crime rate against children in India.

articles.headline: Congress to issue ‘chargesheet’ against Punjab’s AAP govt. soon
articles.summary: Punjab Congress will soon release a 'chargesheet' against the AAP government, alleging rampant corruption and deteriorating law and order. The party plans 'yatras' to connect with people.

articles.headline: Stalin urges new govt. to continue DMK’s welfare schemes
articles.summary: DMK president M.K. Stalin urged the new TN govt. to continue welfare schemes for women, youth, students, and other sections. He thanked alliance partners for supporting TVK.

articles.headline: T.N. heads for coalition govt. as TVK, Cong. seal deal
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu set for its first coalition govt. in decades, with Congress expected to join TVK govt. Congress state in-charge confirmed it will be a coalition government.

articles.headline: Cong. meet fails to solve Kerala CM deadlock
articles.summary: Congress leadership met to resolve Kerala CM deadlock but failed. High command will finalize candidate by May 23. Leaders warned of action against those violating party discipline.

articles.headline: Protests erupt after Dalit bride forced off mare
articles.summary: Dalit bride forced off mare during wedding procession in Udaipur. Protesters allege 12 involved, four arrested. They demand action and a fair investigation, accusing police of inaction.

articles.headline: Tussle over CM fuels meme storm
articles.summary: Tussle for Kerala CM post fuels meme storm on social media. Trolls and memes mock Congress leaders' claims and posturing amid the ongoing leadership debate.

articles.headline: KC(M) blames CPI(M) cross-voting for rout
articles.summary: KC(M) blamed CPI(M) cadres' cross-voting and minority consolidation for LDF's electoral rout in Kerala. They acknowledged the government's development initiatives but cited a lack of popular political face.

articles.headline: Three juveniles sent to observation home in 2024 rape case
articles.summary: Three juveniles, who aided accused in a 2024 rape case in AP, sent to observation home for six months. They admitted to assisting the accused in the offence.

articles.headline: Azim Premji University suspends student over campus violence
articles.summary: Azim Premji University suspended a student for two years and issued warnings to two others following campus violence. Students protested the suspension, demanding its revocation.

articles.headline: Yediyurappa played crucial role in bringing BJP to South: Shah
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah praised B.S. Yediyurappa's role in BJP's growth in South India. He said Yediyurappa deserves golden letters for his contribution to public life.

articles.headline: ED arrests three in 2017 Bitcoin scam in Bengaluru
articles.summary: ED arrested prime accused Srikrishna (Sriki) and two others in Bengaluru Bitcoin scam case. The case relates to a 2017 hacking incident involving alleged theft of bitcoins.

articles.headline: Odisha mob lynching case: 11 held so far
articles.summary: 11 arrested in Odisha mob lynching case where a man was beaten to death, accused of attempted rape. Eyewitness claims assault happened in police presence.

articles.headline: NCP (SP) leader ‘attacked’ with ink by preacher and aides
articles.summary: NCP (SP) leader Vikas Lawande attacked with ink by preacher Sangram Bhandare and aides in Pune. Attack was response to Lawande's 'derogatory remarks' against Hindu spiritual leaders.

articles.headline: Sukhu orders scientific study on recurring cloudbursts
articles.summary: Himachal Pradesh CM Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu ordered scientific study on recurring cloudbursts, examining factors like dams and terrain. ₹1 crore allocated for GLOF studies.

articles.headline: Maharashtra ex-IMA chief held over illegal tests-abortion racket
articles.summary: Former Maharashtra IMA chief Ravindra Kute arrested for alleged illegal tests and abortions. Police have arrested 12 people in connection with the racket.

articles.headline: Rajasthan set to become global hub for agro-based industries, says CM
articles.summary: Rajasthan CM Bhajan Lal Sharma stated the state will become a global hub for agro-based industries, boosting investment and job creation. Focus on food processing and value-added products.

articles.headline: Tiger sighted in Arunachal wildlife sanctuary after nearly two decades
articles.summary: A tiger sighted in Arunachal Pradesh's D’Ering Memorial Wildlife Sanctuary after nearly two decades. Wildlife officials and conservationists hail it as a sign of resilient ecosystem and successful conservation efforts.

articles.headline: Uttarakhand flood maps may be underestimating risk, study warns
articles.summary: Study in Current Science warns Uttarakhand flood maps underestimate risk by relying on average rainfall. Researchers suggest using extreme rainfall scenarios for better hazard assessment.

articles.headline: Hope for pancreatic cancer as new drug shows promise
articles.summary: New drug daraxonrasib shows promise for pancreatic cancer, with potential to shrink tumours. Early results suggest it's effective with mild side-effects. FDA granted 'National Priority Voucher' status.

articles.headline: SBI shoots down Sreeshankar eyes good showing
articles.summary: Sreeshankar aims for good showing at upcoming athletics meets, despite facing WFI's disqualification. He cited training in South Africa and similar weather conditions in Glasgow as beneficial.

articles.headline: Fascinating contest on the cards as CSK meets LSG
articles.summary: Chennai Super Kings host Lucknow Super Giants in an anticipated IPL 2026 clash. Both teams are seeking consistency and playoff qualification, with rain adding to the intrigue.

articles.headline: RCB takes on MI, looks to return to winning ways
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru face Mumbai Indians, aiming to end a two-match losing streak. Venue statistics suggest a low-scoring game. Pandya's fitness remains a concern for MI.

articles.headline: Manchester City stays on Arsenal’s tail
articles.summary: Manchester City beat Brentford 3-0 with goals from Doku, Haaland, and Marmoush, closing the gap on league leaders Arsenal. Liverpool drew with Chelsea.

articles.headline: Williams’ goal helps BFC score over JFC
articles.summary: Ryan Williams scored the winning goal as Bengaluru FC beat Jamshedpur FC 1-0, pushing BFC to fourth place. FC Goa and Mohun Bagan drew 1-1.

articles.headline: The star who stormed into politics
articles.summary: Actor Vijay launched Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) in Feb 2024. TVK secured 108 seats in TN Assembly debut, positioning itself as secular and committed to social justice.

articles.headline: The Bengal pivot
articles.summary: Suvendu Adhikari, Mamata Banerjee's former lieutenant, became West Bengal's first BJP CM. Adhikari, a prominent leader, faced numerous cases after switching parties.

articles.headline: Double-edged sword
articles.summary: Anthropic AI model Mythos can identify and exploit cyber vulnerabilities. IMF warns AI could amplify cyber risks, especially in the financial sector, urging regulation.

articles.headline: Rahul slams Bihar govt. over police action against protesting teacher aspirants
articles.summary: Rahul Gandhi criticized Bihar govt. for lathi-charge on protesting teacher aspirants. He stated youth are fed up with BJP's lies and Congress stands with them.

articles.headline: What is India’s first orbital data centre satellite?
articles.summary: Pixxel partnering with Sarvam to launch Pathfinder, India's first orbital data centre satellite. It will carry GPUs for AI model training and inference in orbit.

articles.headline: Deal hangs in balance amid renewed clashes in the Gulf
articles.summary: Iran questions U.S. diplomacy seriousness amid renewed Gulf clashes. U.S. fighter jet disabled two Iranian tankers. Iran claims it responded to U.S. actions and ceasefire violations.

articles.headline: Rubio defends U.S. sanctions targeting Cuban conglomerate
articles.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended sanctions on Cuba's GAESA conglomerate, calling it a 'military-industrial complex' exploiting the people. Sanctions aim to pressure Havana.

articles.headline: U.K.’s Starmer vows to ‘listen to voters’ after election drubbing
articles.summary: UK PM Keir Starmer promises to 'listen to voters' after Labour's historic election defeat. He admitted the party made 'unnecessary mistakes' and failed to give public 'hope'.

articles.headline: Congo warns of wave of attacks in east
articles.summary: UN mission in DRC warns of 'deadly' wave of attacks in the east targeting civilians. Dozens killed in recent days in Ituri, North Kivu, and South Kivu provinces.

articles.headline: The Hindu’s campaigns shine at INMA Global Media Awards
articles.summary: The Hindu won first place for 'Best Brand Awareness Campaign' and third for 'Best Brand' at INMA Global Media Awards 2026 for its campaigns 'Written By Journalists' and 'Made Of Chennai'.

articles.headline: People celebrate with sweets and colour as ‘Kanthi’s son’ takes oath
articles.summary: Residents of Kanthi celebrated Suvendu Adhikari's swearing-in as CM. His family expressed pride, calling him 'Kanthi's son' and urged him to work for the poor and development.

articles.headline: What is the Governor’s role in a hung Assembly?
articles.summary: Governor appoints CM in hung Assembly, prioritizing stable govt. SC rulings emphasize floor test as objective majority proof, not Governor's discretion. Constitution does not define 'reasonable time'.

articles.headline: Small camera reveals hidden world on Arctic seafloor
articles.summary: Researchers used a portable camera to capture images of life on the Arctic seafloor in a Greenlandic fjord. They observed a bustling ecosystem with various small organisms.

articles.headline: AI tool excels at identifying cells, even ‘new’ ones
articles.summary: AI tool TranscriptFormer identifies cell types with extreme accuracy, even of species it hasn't seen before. It can detect disease states and uncover biological patterns.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity: The need for proactive measures
articles.summary: A study highlights how AI models like Mythos can identify and exploit cyber vulnerabilities, posing risks to financial and other critical sectors. IMF urges proactive regulation.

articles.headline: Caps race is turning heads
articles.summary: IPL 2026 Orange and Purple Cap races are poised for a close finish. No dominant leader in batting or bowling highlights the tournament's unpredictability.

articles.headline: Constructive criticism necessary, says Shedge
articles.summary: PBKS all-rounder Suryansh Shedge values constructive criticism from coaches and captain for improvement. He remained ready for opportunities despite not playing initially.

articles.headline: Never felt I was under pressure due to price tag: Green
articles.summary: KKR's Cameron Green states he never felt pressure due to his high price tag. He credits support staff and constructive criticism for his improved performance this season.

articles.headline: It’s Lux Aeterna vs Promiseofthefuture
articles.summary: Lux Aeterna and Promiseofthefuture are predicted to fight out the finish of the Capricorn Stud Plate race.

articles.headline: SC Order Turns Up Tax Heat on Old Benami Transactions
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that tax authorities can apply the 2016 benami law retrospectively to transactions made before its enactment. This allows for the confiscation of assets and questioning of succession plans used to mask true ownership. The court emphasized the need to unmask attempts to disguise prohibited arrangements through retrospective civil consequences.

articles.headline: Paperwork Causing AI Mission Lag as Startups Flag Funding Concerns
articles.summary: The IndiaAI Mission faces delays as five out of 12 selected startups have yet to sign formal agreements with the government. Concerns include intellectual property rights and the lack of a formal funding mechanism. While compute access has been provided, startups cite operational hurdles and funding uncertainties as major roadblocks.

articles.headline: Register Gig Workers in 45 Days: Govt to Aggregators
articles.summary: The Ministry of Labour and Employment has directed aggregators to register all gig workers on a central portal within 45 days. This move ensures workers are covered under the Code on Social Security, 2020. Aggregators must update records of new appointments and exits in real-time to maintain compliance and facilitate social security benefits.

articles.headline: Iran Playing the Guessing Game on US Peace Plan
articles.summary: Sporadic clashes occurred between Iranian forces and US vessels in the Strait of Hormuz as Tehran evaluates a US-suggested peace plan. While international observers see hope for de-escalation, Iranian officials have remained cautious, labeling CIA assessments of their military resilience as false. Tensions remain high as both sides wait for formal responses.

articles.headline: Auto Inc Turning to Cleaner Fuels but SUVs Chug on Diesel
articles.summary: Despite a shift toward electric and hybrid vehicles, diesel SUVs continue to hold an 18% share of India's passenger vehicle market. While hatchback and sedan buyers are moving away from diesel, the demand remains strong in the SUV segment due to fuel efficiency and long-distance driving capabilities. BS7 emission standards are expected to further impact fuel choices.

articles.headline: Compliance with Labour Laws Must for PSU Contracts
articles.summary: The finance ministry has linked labour law compliance to eligibility for government procurement contracts. Firms can now be debarred from bidding for up to three years for failing to pay wages or make social security contributions. This move aims to protect outsourced and contractual workers in central ministries and public sector enterprises.

articles.headline: Senior Citizens Take to Rental Home Projects
articles.summary: India's senior living market is shifting toward a rental model as the elderly seek flexibility and asset-light living. Projects in cities like Bengaluru and Pune are seeing high demand, with seniors moving from traditional old-age homes to service-led residential units. The shift is driven by rising life expectancy and the growth of nuclear family models.

articles.headline: Trump Awaits Reply; Bahrain Arrests 41 Linked to IRGC
articles.summary: Bahraini authorities have arrested 41 individuals for allegedly planning attacks linked to Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps. This comes as former US President Donald Trump awaits a response to his latest diplomatic proposal to Iran. The arrests heighten regional security concerns amid ongoing tensions over Iran's nuclear program and regional influence.

articles.headline: AMMK Files Complaint Against TVK Over Forged Support Letter
articles.summary: AMMK leader TTV Dhinakaran has filed a police complaint against the TVK party, alleging the use of a forged support letter. The letter purportedly suggested AMMK's support for TVK to form the government in Tamil Nadu. TVK has denied the allegations, claiming the letter is legitimate, as the state prepares for a leadership transition.

articles.headline: AICC Cracks Whip on Kerala Street Lobbying for CM Post
articles.summary: The Congress central leadership has ordered Kerala state leaders to stop public displays of lobbying for the Chief Minister's post. Following a meeting between Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi, the party stated that a decision on the ministerial face would be taken in due course. Leaders have been warned against acts that undermine party discipline.

articles.headline: Modi Honours 97-Year-Old Mukherjee Aide at Bengal Oath
articles.summary: During the swearing-in ceremony in Kolkata, PM Narendra Modi honored 97-year-old Panchanan Bhattacharya, a former aide of Shyama Prasad Mukherjee. The gesture highlighted the BJP's deep historical roots in West Bengal. Bhattacharya, a lifelong nationalist, was recognized for his contributions to the party's ideological foundation in the state.

articles.headline: VCK’s Small Numbers, Big Leverage in TN Politics
articles.summary: Despite having only two MLAs, the VCK has emerged as a kingmaker in Tamil Nadu politics by providing critical support to the TVK. Party leader Thol Thirumavalan stated the decision aims to prevent President's Rule and ensure a stable government. The VCK's move highlights the strategic importance of smaller regional parties in coalition dynamics.

articles.headline: Regional Satraps Rework Strategy After Setbacks
articles.summary: Leaders like Mamata Banerjee and MK Stalin are re-evaluating their political strategies following recent electoral setbacks. With the BJP making inroads in traditional strongholds, regional parties are looking to strengthen the INDIA bloc alliance and address internal dissent. The shift signals a new phase of tactical coordination among opposition parties.

articles.headline: Abducted Tangkhul Woman Released in Manipur
articles.summary: An abducted Tangkhul woman, Olina Ningshen, was released following the intervention of village headmen and local civil society groups in Manipur. She was allegedly taken by a faction of an insurgent group. Her safe return was facilitated after negotiations, and she has since been reunited with her family under the protection of security forces.

articles.headline: BPF, AGP Likely to Get 1 Berth Each in Assam
articles.summary: The Bodoland People's Front and Asom Gana Parishad are likely to be allotted one cabinet berth each in the upcoming Assam government expansion. BJP leadership is currently finalizing the council of ministers to balance regional and ally interests. The expansion is expected to accommodate key representatives from the NDA partner parties.

articles.headline: UP Tells Bureaucrats to Stand Up for MPs, MLAs
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government has issued a directive to bureaucrats to strictly follow protocol when meeting MPs and MLAs. This follows complaints from lawmakers about being ignored or disrespected by district officials. The order mandates that officials must stand to receive legislators and offer them water upon arrival to ensure institutional decorum.

articles.headline: Jan Suraksha Enrolments Near 940m as Govt Highlights Benefits
articles.summary: Total enrolments under the government's flagship Jan Suraksha schemes, including life and accident insurance, have reached nearly 940 million. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman noted that claims worth ₹21,500 crore have been settled under PMJJBY. The schemes aim to provide a low-cost social security net to underserved sections of the population.

articles.headline: Amazon Sub-leases Warehousing Space in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Amazon India has sub-leased approximately 278,000 square feet of warehousing space in Bengaluru's Nelamangala area from AGP. The agreement includes a monthly rental of over ₹72 lakh. This move reflects Amazon's strategy to optimize its logistics footprint and enhance its e-commerce fulfillment capabilities in southern India.

articles.headline: Pernod Ricard Faces CCI Ire Over Exclusive Retail Deals
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India has initiated an investigation into Pernod Ricard for alleged anti-competitive arrangements in Delhi. The French liquor giant is accused of entering exclusive deals with retailers to maintain a 35% market share. The probe follows complaints from a rival liquor manufacturer regarding market distortion and entry barriers.

articles.headline: M&M Diversifying its Portfolio Beyond Diesel SUVs
articles.summary: Mahindra & Mahindra is expanding its product portfolio to include electric and hybrid powertrains, moving away from its traditional diesel-heavy focus. While diesel remains a stronghold in the SUV segment, the company is investing in new technologies to meet evolving emission standards. The move aims to capture a wider share of the luxury and mass-market PV segments.

articles.headline: Show of Strength: Russian Jets Over Red Square
articles.summary: Russian Air Force Su-25 jets flew over Moscow's Red Square, trailing smoke in the colors of the national flag during the Victory Day military parade. The event showcased Russia's air power and military hardware amidst ongoing international tensions. The parade is an annual display of national pride and military capabilities.

articles.headline: Gen Ex Gets the Gadgets; Tech Struggles for Seniors
articles.summary: The article explores the humorous and often chaotic attempts of the older generation to keep up with modern technology and AI. It highlights how seniors use smartphones and social media to stay connected, while often falling prey to algorithms or accidental double-purchases. The piece reflects on the changing social dynamics in a hyper-connected world.

articles.headline: Why There Can't Be a 'Great Indian Novel'
articles.summary: This literary analysis argues that India's diverse and heterogeneous nature makes it impossible to produce a single 'state-of-the-nation' novel. Instead, the author suggests that Indian literature excels at regional and community-focused storytelling. The piece cites various authors who have successfully captured local nuances rather than a singular national identity.

articles.headline: Nepo, the Reassuring Story of Ourselves
articles.summary: The feature discusses the public's fascination and frustration with 'nepotism babies' in the entertainment industry. It argues that celebrity dynasties provide a sense of familiar narrative in a fragmented world. Using examples from Bollywood and international pop culture, it explores how inherited fame remains a constant topic of public discourse and social media.

articles.headline: Let's Talk About... Sloppers: AI Content Fatigue
articles.summary: The rise of 'sloppers'—individuals who produce low-effort, AI-generated content—is becoming a concern for digital quality. The article examines how the glut of mediocre AI content on social media and news feeds is leading to user fatigue. It calls for better detection mechanisms and a return to high-quality, human-curated information.

articles.headline: Title Clasico: Barcelona vs Real Madrid for La Liga
articles.summary: Barcelona and Real Madrid face off in a critical Clasico that could decide the La Liga title. Leading by 11 points, Hansi Flick's Barcelona could be crowned champions with a victory. Real Madrid, aiming for their 37th title, needs a win to keep the race alive. The match at Camp Nou is expected to be a high-stakes encounter for both clubs.

articles.headline: Djokovic Falls in Second Round to Qualifier Prizmic
articles.summary: World No. 1 Novak Djokovic was knocked out of the Italian Open in the second round by 18-year-old Croatian qualifier Dino Prizmic. The 6-3, 3-6, 6-4 loss marks a significant upset for Djokovic, who was returning to the clay court after a hiatus. Prizmic's victory is seen as a major breakthrough for the young tennis prospect.

articles.headline: Smartphone Memory Problem: India's Growing Data Epidemic
articles.summary: India is facing a data obesity epidemic as smartphone storage fails to keep up with the increasing volume of high-resolution photos and 4K videos. By 2030, India could have 100 million smartphone users paying for cloud storage. Tech giants are bundling AI features and content with storage plans to capitalize on this growing consumer need.

articles.headline: Show of Hands: Skincare Trend Focuses on Extremities
articles.summary: Handcare is becoming the next major trend in the beauty industry, with a focus on specialized treatments for the hands. Influenced by social media and anti-aging concerns, consumers are increasingly using products like retinol, dermal fillers, and sun protection for their hands. The segment is evolving from basic routine to specialized dermatological care.

articles.headline: Rx Made-in-China for Cancer: Low-Cost Immunotherapies
articles.summary: Chinese-made immunotherapies for cancer are offering a more affordable alternative for Indian patients, with prices up to 75% lower than western MNC drugs. Pharma giants like Zydus and Glenmark are partnering with Chinese firms to bring these life-saving treatments to India. The shift could significantly reduce out-of-pocket expenses for Indian cancer patients.

articles.headline: The Twain Met at the Met Gala: Indian Craftsmanship
articles.summary: Indian traditional craftsmanship, featuring techniques like shola, kalamkari, and zardozi, was showcased at the Met Gala in New York. Designers like Amit Aggarwal and Sabyasachi integrated these heritage skills into high-fashion ensembles, highlighting the global appeal of Indian artisans. The event underscored a shift toward recognizing manual skill as luxury fashion.

articles.headline: CBI Searches 17 ADAG-Linked Sites in Mumbai and Delhi
articles.summary: The CBI conducted searches at 17 locations in Mumbai and Delhi related to cases against the Reliance ADA Group. Investigations involve Reliance Telecom Ltd, Reliance Commercial Finance, and Reliance Home Finance. Officials examined residential and office premises of directors following warrants issued by a special CBI court regarding alleged diversion of bank funds.

articles.headline: US and Iran May Resume Nuclear Negotiations Next Week
articles.summary: Diplomatic sources suggest the United States and Iran are preparing to restart month-long negotiations in Islamabad next week. Mediators from the EU have been working on a framework for the talks, which aim to address long-standing tensions and sanctions relief. Both sides have signaled cautious interest in diplomatic re-engagement despite regional instability.

articles.headline: DRDO Successfully Tests Agni Missile with Multiple Re-entry Vehicle Technology
articles.summary: India's DRDO successfully flight-tested the Agni missile with Multiple Independently Targetable Re-entry Vehicle (MIRV) technology from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island. The test, named 'Mission Divyastra,' allows a single missile to deploy multiple nuclear warheads to different targets. This achievement significantly enhances India's strategic deterrence capabilities.

articles.headline: MSMEs Face 30% Losses Due to Surging Industrial LPG Costs
articles.summary: Small and medium industries in Bengaluru are struggling as industrial LPG prices have risen nearly 50% per metric tonne since March. Over 24% of units in the Peenya industrial hub are affected, with production costs increasing by 20-30%. Industrialists have appealed to the government for immediate price relief and an extension of GST payment deadlines.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Protects Shashi Tharoor's Personality and Image Rights
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has granted an interim injunction to Congress MP Shashi Tharoor, protecting his personality and publicity rights. The court ordered the removal of AI-generated deepfake videos showing Tharoor in compromising positions. The ruling bars anyone from using his voice, likeness, or signature for commercial or political purposes without consent.

articles.headline: Customs Data Leak Probe: Police FIR Filed Over Sensitive Data Sale
articles.summary: Delhi Police have filed an FIR following a breach of sensitive data from the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs. The data, including importer-exporter details and shipping links, was reportedly being sold on over 70 websites. Investigators are probing an 'insider' role and looking into various international platforms where sample datasets were published.

articles.headline: India to Host African Leaders at Upcoming Infrastructure Summit
articles.summary: India is set to host the 4th India-Africa Summit, aiming to ramp up development cooperation in areas like agriculture, health, and technology. Over 40 heads of state are expected to participate. Prime Minister Modi has already fulfilled $10 billion in previous credit commitments, and new major initiatives in infrastructure and digital connectivity are expected.

articles.headline: Hantavirus Outbreak Reported on Remote Spanish and Atlantic Islands
articles.summary: Health authorities have reported three deaths and several infections from Hantavirus on the Spanish island of La Palma and the remote Tristan da Cunha. The outbreak is believed to have started among passengers of a luxury cruise liner. Health officials are monitoring former passengers across South Africa, the Netherlands, and Switzerland to contain further spread.

articles.headline: PM-JAY to Use AI to Combat Healthcare Insurance Fraud
articles.summary: Officials from the Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana are exploring the use of AI tools to detect fabricated medical records and fake insurance claims. Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science have developed systems that identify patterns of healthcare deception, such as altered clinical notes and patient photos. This initiative aims to protect the integrity of the insurance scheme.

articles.headline: NCRB Crime Rates Skewed Due to Outdated Census Data
articles.summary: A mathematical discrepancy has emerged in NCRB crime data because it still relies on 2011 Census population figures for calculations. This has led to inflated crime rates in rapidly growing cities. Experts warn that crime per lakh population figures will remain inaccurate until the next official Census is conducted, as the denominator used for calculations is significantly outdated.

articles.headline: Kuki Rebels Enter Manipur Conflict Raising Border Violence Concerns
articles.summary: Violence in Manipur's border regions has intensified as ethnic Kuki militants have reportedly entered the conflict. Armed clashes have occurred near the Indo-Myanmar border, leading to several casualties and the displacement of local villagers. Security forces have been put on high alert to prevent further cross-border movement of insurgents and to restore order in the sensitive area.

articles.headline: FPI Stake in Indian Equities Hits 14-Year Low; DIIs Overtake
articles.summary: Foreign Portfolio Investors' (FPI) stake in Indian equities fell to 14.7% in April 2026, the lowest since June 2012. Domestic Institutional Investors (DII) increased their share to 18.9%, surpassing FPIs for the first time since 2024. FPIs withdrew ₹14,207 crore in early May as funds shifted to markets like Taiwan and South Korea.

articles.headline: Suvendu Adhikari Sworn in as West Bengal CM, Ending TMC Rule
articles.summary: BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari became West Bengal's Chief Minister, marking the end of 15 years of TMC rule. The BJP secured 207 seats in the Assembly elections. Prime Minister Narendra Modi and other NDA leaders attended the oath ceremony in Kolkata, where Adhikari vowed to rebuild the state.

articles.headline: TVK Chief Joseph Vijay to Take Oath as Tamil Nadu CM Today
articles.summary: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) leader C. Joseph Vijay will be sworn in as Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister. He secured support from VCK and IUML, enabling his party to cross the majority mark. The Governor has asked Vijay to prove his majority by May 13.

articles.headline: PM Modi to Launch ₹1,535 Crore Railway Projects in Telangana
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate railway infrastructure projects worth ₹1,535 crore in Telangana. Key projects include an 118-km multi-tracking on the Kazipet–Vijayawada section and a new Kazipet rail-under-rail bypass to improve freight traffic and reduce congestion.

articles.headline: India Plans to Intensify Trade, Investment Cooperation with Africa
articles.summary: India will host the 4th India-Africa Forum Summit this month-end, focusing on agriculture, defense, green energy, and digital partnerships. India is a top trade partner for Africa, with bilateral trade reaching $82 billion in FY25 and cumulative Indian investments at $80 billion.

articles.headline: Lt Gen Raja Subramani Appointed Next Chief of Defence Staff
articles.summary: Lt Gen N S Raja Subramani will be the next Chief of Defence Staff, succeeding General Anil Chauhan on May 30. Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan has been named the next Chief of Naval Staff. Subramani is known for his expertise in defense procurement and indigenous manufacturing.

articles.headline: Stalin Slams Congress for Severing Ties with DMK
articles.summary: MK Stalin criticized Congress for abruptly ending its alliance with DMK. He praised Left parties and VCK for supporting TVK to form the government, emphasizing the importance of respecting allied parties' decisions for stable governance.

articles.headline: Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora Arrested by ED in Money Laundering Probe
articles.summary: Enforcement Directorate (ED) arrested Punjab Industries Minister Sanjeev Arora in a money laundering case. The AAP labeled the arrest as politically motivated, while the BJP suggested Arora had refused to join their party. The ED's action has sparked political debate.

articles.headline: Putin Slams NATO in Victory Day Speech
articles.summary: During Russia's scaled-back Victory Day parade, President Vladimir Putin stated that Russian soldiers in Ukraine are fighting an 'aggressive force' backed by NATO. He described his war objectives in Ukraine as 'just'.

articles.headline: India Tests Advanced Agni-5 Missile with MIRV Technology
articles.summary: India successfully conducted a flight test of the Agni-5 missile equipped with Multiple Independently targetable Reentry Vehicle (MIRV) technology. This capability allows the missile to deliver multiple nuclear warheads to separate targets simultaneously, significantly enhancing India's strategic deterrence.

articles.headline: Two Men Returning from IPL Match Killed by Speeding Car in Delhi
articles.summary: Two men, on their way back from an IPL match in Delhi, were killed in a speeding car accident in Bhajanpura. The driver of the car has been arrested, and the police are investigating the incident. Neither victim was reportedly wearing a helmet.

articles.headline: Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Passengers Fear Stigmatization
articles.summary: Passengers aboard a cruise ship affected by hantavirus are concerned about facing hostility and stigmatization upon their return home, fueled by sensational media reports and online reactions.

articles.headline: Govt Names Next CDS, Navy Chief Amid Theaterization Push
articles.summary: The government has appointed Lt Gen NS Raja Subramani as the next Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) and Vice Adm Krishna Swaminathan as the next Chief of Naval Staff. These appointments coincide with the armed forces' drive towards integrated theater commands.

articles.headline: CJI Surya Kant Rejects 'Casteist Statement' Allegations
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant strongly condemned a 'vile, brazen, and mischievous' casteist statement attributed to him, labeling it a 'fictitious concoction' and an act of dishonesty. He urged citizens not to amplify false content.

articles.headline: Hacker Among 3 Held in Crypto-Linked PMLA Case
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) arrested three individuals, including hacker Srikrishna Ramesh, in connection with a multi-crore Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) case involving cryptocurrency. The investigation centers on the alleged siphoning of funds into digital wallets.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee Urges Opposition to Unite Against BJP
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee called for a united opposition front to counter the BJP, emphasizing the need for a collective platform at both central and state levels. She also hinted at potential legal challenges following the election results.

articles.headline: BJP's First Bengal Cabinet Balances Regional and Caste Representation
articles.summary: West Bengal's new BJP cabinet includes veteran leaders, first-generation converts, and representatives from Matua, tribal, and Rajbanshi communities. Agnimitra Paul is the sole woman minister, reflecting a carefully crafted cross-section of society.

articles.headline: LG Orders Restoration of 77 Water Bodies Across Delhi
articles.summary: Delhi's Lieutenant Governor has directed the Delhi Development Authority (DDA) to restore 77 water bodies in a phased manner. The plan involves dredging, clearing catchment areas, and beautification to improve water resources.

articles.headline: Gill and Rashid Star as Titans Thrash Royals by 77 Runs
articles.summary: Gujarat Titans defeated Rajasthan Royals by 77 runs, securing their fourth consecutive victory. Shubman Gill scored 84 and Sai Sudharsan added 55, while Rashid Khan took 4 wickets to restrict the Royals.

articles.headline: Yashasvi Jaiswal, Shafali Verma Issued Notices by NADA for Missed Dope Tests
articles.summary: Cricketers Yashasvi Jaiswal and Shafali Verma have received notices from the National Anti-Doping Agency (NADA) for failing to update their whereabouts information, leading to missed dope tests. Three such failures within a year can result in a ban.

articles.headline: Sahil Jadhav Wins India's First Archery World Cup Bronze
articles.summary: Archer Sahil Jadhav secured India's first-ever medal at the Archery World Cup, winning bronze in the compound men's event. The 25-year-old defeated Denmark's Martin Damsbo in the playoff match, reaching a career-high world ranking of 11th.

articles.headline: Peter Magyar Sworn in as Hungary's Prime Minister
articles.summary: Peter Magyar has been sworn in as Hungary's new Prime Minister, marking the end of Viktor Orban's 16-year rule. Magyar's Tisza party achieved a significant victory over Fidesz, and he has pledged to combat corruption and reform institutions.

articles.headline: Israeli Strikes Kill 17 in Lebanon; Hezbollah Claims Drone Attack
articles.summary: Israeli drone strikes in southern Lebanon have killed at least 17 people, including a father and daughter. Hezbollah reported firing a drone at an Israeli military post amidst escalating tensions. The attacks occurred south of Beirut.

articles.headline: Public Health Advisory Issued; Infrastructure Upgraded
articles.summary: A new public health advisory has been issued with guidelines on current health concerns. Concurrently, there are updates on public health infrastructure, including investments in new hospitals and clinics nationwide.

articles.headline: National Security Briefing Covers Policy and Regional Stability
articles.summary: A briefing on national security provides insights into recent developments, strategic updates, military preparedness, and regional dialogues. It covers policy updates and defense initiatives.

articles.headline: Innovations in Science and Agriculture Reported
articles.summary: Recent breakthroughs in science and technology are highlighted, alongside innovations in agricultural science, including new farming techniques and crop genetic improvements aimed at enhancing food security.

articles.headline: Judicial Reforms Focus on Procedures and Backlog Reduction
articles.summary: Discussions are underway regarding judicial reforms, focusing on changes in legal procedures, court infrastructure upgrades, and efforts to reduce case backlogs. The potential implications of these changes for the legal system are being analyzed.

articles.headline: Manufacturing Sector Sees Growth, Expansion, and Policy Updates
articles.summary: The manufacturing sector is experiencing growth, with reports on production increases, investment opportunities, and expansion including new industrial zones. New policies aim to boost domestic production and exports.

articles.headline: Rural, Skill, and National Development Programs Highlighted
articles.summary: Various programs are underway to improve rural livelihoods and infrastructure, enhance youth employability through skill development, and promote national development focused on poverty reduction and employment.

articles.headline: International Trade Agreements and Disputes Discussed
articles.summary: New international trade agreements are detailed, exploring their economic impact and market access. Separately, ongoing trade disputes and their implications for global supply chains are being addressed.

articles.headline: Wildlife and Biodiversity Conservation Efforts Detailed
articles.summary: Ongoing efforts in wildlife and biodiversity conservation are reported, focusing on protected areas, endangered species, and national park initiatives. Community involvement is highlighted.

articles.headline: Higher Education Reforms and Funding Updates Provided
articles.summary: Proposed reforms in higher education aim to change curriculum and accreditation. Simultaneously, reports detail funding for institutions, discussing grants and endowments for research.

articles.headline: Digital Economy and Payment Systems Show Rapid Growth
articles.summary: The digital economy is experiencing rapid growth, driven by e-commerce and digital payments. Fintech adoption is increasing, with new platforms and security features enhancing financial inclusion.

articles.headline: Healthcare Access Improved in Underserved Areas
articles.summary: Efforts to improve healthcare access in rural and underserved areas include new clinics, mobile units, and telemedicine. Health awareness campaigns are also being conducted.

articles.headline: International Relations and Aid Dialogue Continues
articles.summary: Developments in international relations include diplomatic engagements and bilateral agreements. International aid and development partnerships focus on collaborative projects and humanitarian efforts.

articles.headline: Digital Literacy and Media Literacy Programs Expand
articles.summary: Digital literacy campaigns aim to bridge the technology gap and promote safe internet use. Digital media literacy programs combat misinformation and foster critical thinking online.

articles.headline: Public-Private Partnerships Drive Development in Key Sectors
articles.summary: Public-private partnerships are playing a key role in national development, with successful collaborations noted in infrastructure, healthcare, and education. Specialized models are explored for healthcare.

articles.headline: Renewable Energy Projects and Policies Advance Sustainability
articles.summary: New renewable energy projects, including solar and wind initiatives, are underway, supported by policies offering incentives. These efforts aim to achieve sustainable energy generation goals.

articles.headline: Consumer Protection Regulations and Rights Enforcement Strengthened
articles.summary: New consumer protection regulations are in place to safeguard rights against unfair practices. Consumer rights awareness campaigns and grievance mechanisms are also enhanced.

articles.headline: Cultural Heritage Preservation and Tourism Initiatives Underway
articles.summary: Efforts for cultural heritage preservation include restoration projects and archaeological discoveries. Cultural heritage tourism is being promoted as an economic driver.

articles.headline: Road and Rural Infrastructure Development Projects Progress
articles.summary: Road infrastructure development includes new highway constructions and widening projects. Rural road connectivity is improving with new constructions and upgrades in remote regions.

articles.headline: International Aid and Development Partnerships Focus on Global Goals
articles.summary: International aid and development partnerships focus on collaborative projects and humanitarian efforts to address global challenges. Discussions cover shared goals for prosperity and stability.

articles.headline: Technology Adoption in Education, Health, and Industry Increases
articles.summary: Technology adoption is rising in education (e-learning), health (telemedicine), and industry (automation, IoT). Advanced technologies drive efficiency and innovation across sectors.

articles.headline: Health Insurance Reforms Aim to Boost Coverage and Affordability
articles.summary: Proposed health insurance reforms seek to increase coverage and affordability, with government schemes and private sector participation highlighted. Expansion targets underserved populations.

articles.headline: Sports Infrastructure Projects and Rural Projects Progress
articles.summary: New sports infrastructure, including stadiums and training centers, is being developed. Simultaneously, rural infrastructure projects like roads and irrigation facilities are progressing.

articles.headline: Judicial Independence Debates and Training Programs Continue
articles.summary: Debates surrounding judicial independence and reforms to strengthen the legal system are ongoing. Concurrently, training programs for judges focus on new legal frameworks and efficiency.

articles.headline: Banking Sector Performance and Innovations Tracked
articles.summary: The banking sector shows steady performance with loan growth and improved asset quality. Innovations in digital banking and fintech integration are enhancing customer experience.

articles.headline: Economic Growth Projections and Stimulus Packages Announced
articles.summary: New economic growth projections anticipate key indicators and investment trends. Government stimulus packages aim to boost consumption, investment, and job creation across sectors.

articles.headline: Judicial and Vocational Training Programs Update
articles.summary: Training programs for judges focus on legal frameworks and efficiency. New vocational programs equip youth with job-ready skills through industry partnerships and apprenticeships.

articles.headline: Digital Inclusion Programs Target All Age Groups
articles.summary: Digital inclusion programs aim for equitable technology access, covering internet penetration, digital skills training, and affordable devices. Specific programs cater to the elderly.

articles.headline: Environmental Conservation Policies and Legislation Strengthened
articles.summary: New environmental conservation policies and legislation are being implemented, focusing on forest protection, biodiversity, and sustainable land use. Stricter regulations are introduced.

articles.headline: Financial and Industrial Sector Reforms Detailed
articles.summary: Reforms in the financial sector aim for market efficiency, while industrial sector reforms focus on boosting production, investment, and exports through policy changes and incentives.

articles.headline: Centre Operationalises Four Labour Codes, Trade Unions Protest
articles.summary: The Centre has operationalised four Labour Codes, replacing 29 laws. Trade unions are protesting, claiming their suggestions were ignored and that the codes are an attack on workers' rights.

articles.headline: Union Minister's Son Faces POCSO Case in Telangana; Counter-Complaint Filed
articles.summary: Telangana Police have booked Union Minister Bandi Sanjay's son, Bandi Bhageerath, under POCSO for alleged sexual assault. The family filed a counter-complaint alleging a 'honey-trap' and extortion attempt.

articles.headline: Growers Urge Status Quo on Import Duty for Natural Rubber
articles.summary: The United Planters’ Association has requested the government to maintain the import duty on natural rubber. Indian rubber prices are subdued compared to international prices due to rising input costs.

articles.headline: Biocon Founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw Announces Succession Plan
articles.summary: Biocon founder Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw has announced a succession plan, appointing her niece Claire as her successor. Biocon, which began as a garage venture, has grown into a global biotech leader.

articles.headline: Techno Sportswear to Expand to Odisha with New Garment Unit
articles.summary: Activewear manufacturer Techno Sportswear plans to expand to Odisha by setting up a garmenting unit. The company aims to leverage the state's port facilities, workforce, and raw material availability.

articles.headline: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Raises Global Concern
articles.summary: An outbreak of hantavirus on the cruise ship MV Hondius has caused global concern, with three deaths reported. The WHO clarified that human-to-human transmission is limited, but warned of severe respiratory illness.

articles.headline: Indian Sailor Killed in Dhow Fire Near Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: An Indian sailor died and four others were injured when their dhow caught fire and capsized near the Strait of Hormuz. Seventeen crew members were rescued by a passing vessel amid regional tensions.

articles.headline: IMF Approves $1.32 Billion Funding for Pakistan
articles.summary: The IMF board has approved a $1.32 billion disbursement for Pakistan following a review of economic reform commitments. The funding includes $1.1 billion under the Extended Fund Facility and aims to stabilize the nation's economy.

articles.headline: WFI Bars Vinesh Phogat from Competition Due to Disciplinary Violations
articles.summary: The Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) has barred Vinesh Phogat from competing until June 26 and issued a show-cause notice, citing indiscipline and contract violations. She will miss upcoming trials and tournaments.
